lines.text: (low) [quietly] Forty-two oh six. Take off twenty-one forty.
[deliberate] Tonight I am signing the Lunar Heritage Neutralization Accord.
(then) Fifty-seven years ago, one nation planted its banner on ground that belongs to all mankind. [pause] It stayed there through every apology we owed and never gave.
(then) Our own scientists tell us fifty years of sun have bleached all six of them to white. [slowly] There is no color left in them at all.
Some call those flags of surrender. [pause] I call them flags of peace.
(then) This coming July fourth, we will bring all six of them home, live, with one world watching. [deliberate] Not Independence Day. This year, the first Unity Day.
(then) And then we give them away. One flag to each nation this country has wronged. [pause] The first, to the people of Iran.
(then) In their place, our crew will plant the Harmony Banner, [slowly] designed for us by a committee of forty-four nations.
You've got the old salute there on the one side, and beside it an artist's rendering of the same flag, boiled white. [pause] They're calling it the most generous act in the history of spaceflight.
